constraints: wants an AI micro-SaaS in 30 days
credibility_signals: technical ability to prototype and ship
unfair_advantages: proximity to common developer workflow pain as a builder

binding_constraints: only about 16 hours total in 30 days

opportunities.title: AI-assisted developer workflow kit for one boring pain
opportunities.thesis: Sell a tiny developer workflow pack through an existing checkout or marketplace to test whether a narrow pain converts without calls.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: Fast to ship, no need for calls, and can be based on firsthand developer pain.
opportunities.why_this_could_fail: Weak edge, weak defensibility, and low distribution make real revenue unlikely.
validation_plan: Review 50 public pain signals and collect 20 exact phrases.
Publish one landing page with pricing by day 2.
Get at least 30 visits and 5 signups or 2 checkout attempts in 7 days.
kill_criteria: Fewer than 30 qualified visits after 7 days
Zero purchase intent signals after launch

opportunities.title: GitHub app for CI failure triage and recurring failure digests
opportunities.thesis: Build a tiny AI-assisted developer workflow product with a narrow promise and distribute it through existing developer channels.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user can build it solo and likely understands the pain from personal experience.
opportunities.why_this_could_fail: There is no strong moat, distribution is still hard, and 30 days is too short for dependable revenue.
validation_plan: Choose one pain only: CI failure triage.
Publish a priced landing page and one working demo within 7 days.
Target 3 paying users, or 10 qualified signups, or 5 activated users with 2 stated willingness-to-pay signals.
kill_criteria: Fewer than 3 paying users after 30 days
Zero activation repeat usage after 14 days
Cannot explain the user and workflow in one sentence

opportunities.title: Async build-for-one developer workflow tool, then productize
opportunities.thesis: Solve one painful workflow for a few real users through async setup, then convert the repeated workflow into product software.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: This is the fastest route to real demand learning and better future product fit.
opportunities.why_this_could_fail: It conflicts with the user's stated refusal to do service work and still requires some outreach.
validation_plan: Complete 3 manual or semi-manual pilot setups.
Only productize if the same workflow repeats at least twice.
kill_criteria: User remains unwilling to do any service-like setup
No repeated pain across the first 3 pilots

rejected_paths.title: Generic AI wrapper for a broad audience
rejected_paths.reason: No differentiation, no audience, and no cheap acquisition path.

rejected_paths.title: Content-first AI business
rejected_paths.reason: Conflicts with the user's preference and lacks an existing audience.

rejected_paths.title: Broad freelance AI implementation
rejected_paths.reason: Fastest route to cash, but conflicts with no services and no sales calls.

next_actions: Treat the 30-day goal as validation, not reliable income.
Pick one narrow developer workflow pain and ignore everything else.
Ship a priced landing page by day 2 and a minimal demo by day 3 or 4.
List the product in at least 3 existing channels.
Kill the idea fast if there is no activation or buying intent.
